rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical cobalt-catalyzed asymmetric hydroacylation of 1,6-enynes with biaryl dialdehydes by a desymmetrization strategy, constructing remote dual stereocenters (central/axial, central/planar chirality).
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of [(S,S)-BDPP]Co(II)Br2 precatalyst to a low-valent chiral Co(I) active species (E1/2 Co(II)/Co(I) = -0.56 V by CV); current is required for catalyst turn-on/regeneration.

key_intermediates.name: Co(III) metallacycle from enantioselective oxidative cyclization of 1,6-enyne (Int-II)
Co(III) cobaltacycle after migratory insertion of dialdehyde C-H/C=O (Int-III)
Co(III)-alkyl/acyl species prior to β-hydride elimination (Int-IV)
Diene byproduct 10 from enyne + EtOH (cobaltacycle protonation product)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Co(II)Br2/BDPP to Co(I), which oxidatively cyclizes the 1,6-enyne to a Co(III) metallacycle (sets first stereocenter); migratory insertion of the prochiral dialdehyde sets the second stereocenter, then β-hydride elimination and C-C reductive elimination release the ketone product and regenerate Co(I).
rate_determining_step: Not C-H cleavage of the aldehyde (kH/kD ≈ 1.0); turnover-limiting step is proposed to be the oxidative cyclization / migratory insertion stage rather than aldehyde C-H activation.

probe_reagents.name: D-1a (90% D-labeled dialdehyde)
probe_reagents.observation: Product D-3a obtained with 90% D incorporation, confirming aldehyde C-H is transferred to the alkene carbon of the product.

probe_reagents.name: D-1a + 1c crossover mixture
probe_reagents.observation: Deuterium incorporated exclusively into D-3a (from D-1a), no crossover to 3q; rules out intermolecular ligand-to-ligand hydrogen transfer (intramolecular pathway).

probe_reagents.name: 1a + D-1a parallel KIE
probe_reagents.observation: kH/kD ≈ 1.0, indicating C-H cleavage of the aldehyde is not rate-limiting.

probe_reagents.name: Ethanol (proton source) with enyne 2a
probe_reagents.observation: Affords diene 10 (31%, 38% e.e.), trapping the cobaltacycle and supporting an oxidative cyclization pathway.

probe_reagents.name: Truncated substrates 11 (no alkene) and 12 (no alkyne)
probe_reagents.observation: No hydroacylation products formed, indicating both π-components are needed for oxidative cyclization (consistent with cobaltacycle Int-II).

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of [(S,S)-BDPP]CoBr2
probe_reagents.observation: Co(II)/Co(I) couple at -0.56 V supports cathodic generation of low-valent Co(I) under the applied current.

probe_reagents.name: Control without current
probe_reagents.observation: No product; electricity is essential, consistent with electrocatalytic Co(I) generation.

probe_reagents.name: X-ray diffraction of 3a and 9a
probe_reagents.observation: Confirms absolute configuration of axial/planar-central chirality products.

probe_reagents.name: DFT rotational barrier of C-C axis in 3a

notes: An alternative ligand-to-ligand hydrogen transfer (LLHT) pathway was explicitly excluded by the crossover experiment. The catalyst exerts dual stereocontrol in two distinct elementary steps: oxidative cyclization sets the central stereocenter on the pyrrolidine, while migratory insertion into the prochiral dialdehyde sets the remote C-C axial / C-O axial / [2.2]paracyclophane planar / ferrocene planar chirality (desymmetrization). The proposal closely parallels Lautens's Co-catalyzed hydroacylation cycle.
